research_scope: ToolRegistry and SessionTools subsystem — definition, registration flow, MCP entry, unregistration, Effect service wrapping

methods.name: named
methods.impl_note: Returns named {task, read} from InstanceState. Lines 773-776

methods.name: tools
methods.impl_note: Filters all() by provider capability, runtime flags, and performs plugin.trigger('tool.definition') for each. Lines 726-770

interface.no_register_method: Interface has no register/unregister/addTool method. Tools are fully defined at layer construction time.

state.type: State = { custom: Tool.Def[]; modeDescriptions: Map<string, Record<string, string>>; builtin: Tool.Def[]; task: Tool.Def; read: Tool.Def }
state.scoping: Keyed by InstanceContext.directory via ScopedCache. Tied to Effect Scope lifecycle — disposed when scope closes or InstanceStore.dispose() is called.

dependencies: All builtin tool classes (yield* for each)

registration_phases.description: Calls Tool.init(toolInstance) for every builtin tool. (~80 tools total). Stored in a flat tool object keyed by snake_case ID.

Spreads all initialized tools into a builtin array. Conditionally includes/excludes based on runtime flags: experimentalScout, experimentalLspTool, experimentalPlanMode, experimentalCoordination, experimentalDuckDBQuery. Question tool is conditionally included.

Glob-scans {tool,tools}/*.{js,ts} in config directories. Dynamically imports each file and pushes ToolDefinitions into custom[]. Uses fromPlugin() to convert. Checks BUILTIN_TOOL_IDS for ID conflicts (appends '_plugin' suffix).
Iterates plugin.list(). For each plugin, checks capability 'tool.register' via plugin.checkCapability(). For each entry in p.tool, creates a safe ID (prefixed plugin-<id>) and pushes into custom[].
Calls ToolGraph.buildGraph() after all tools registered.

builtin_tool_ids.purpose: Used to detect naming conflicts with user/plugin tools. If a custom tool matches a builtin name, '_plugin' suffix is appended.

default_layer_composition.description: defaultLayer = layer.pipe(Layer.provide(...)) with 20+ layer dependencies including Config, Plugin, Question, Todo, Skill, Agent, Session, Provider, Git, Reference, LSP, Instruction, AppFileSystem, Bus, FetchHttpClient, Format, CrossSpawnSpawner, Ripgrep, Truncate, ToolCache, DuckDBConfig, DatabaseConfig, DatabaseAdapter, DuckDB, Storage, RuntimeFlags, EventStore, EventAgentQueries, FileMemory

session_tools.not_a_service: SessionTools is NOT an Effect Service. It's a namespace export with a single Effect.fn.

primary_function.name: resolve
primary_function.sig: Effect.fn(...)(function* (input: { agent, model, session, processor, bypassAgentCheck, messages, promptOps }))
primary_function.description: Bridge layer that reads ToolRegistry (built-in) and MCP (external), wraps each with runtime concerns (semaphores, hooks, caching, tracing, truncation), and produces ai.Tool objects for LLM generation.

relationship_to_tool_registry.role: Bridge/adapter — reads from both registries, enriches with runtime behavior, outputs unified ai.Tool record

builtin_tools.semaphore: readSemaphore (10 permits) for cacheable, writeSemaphore (3 permits) for non-cacheable
builtin_tools.attachment_annotation: Adds PartID.ascending(), sessionID, messageID to each attachment

mcp_tools.content_parsing: Handles text, image (base64), resource (text/blob) content types

tool_registration_flow.description: MCP tools are NOT registered in ToolRegistry. They live in MCP's own InstanceState.

state.type: State = { config, status, clients: Record<string, MCPClient>, defs: Record<string, MCPToolDef[]> }

flow: 1. MCP layer initialization: reads config.mcp, creates clients for each configured server
2. For each connected server, calls defs() → listTools() → converts via convertMcpTool()
4. Registers ToolListChangedNotificationSchema handler via watch() for dynamic updates
5. SessionTools.resolve() iterates yield* mcp.tools() which reads s.defs and converts to ai.Tool

tool_list_changed.handler: watch() function (line 531) — on notification, re-lists tools and updates s.defs[name]

connect_disconnect.disconnect: closeClient(s, name) + delete s.clients[name] + delete s.status[name] — lines 712-723
connect_disconnect.close_client: Deletes s.defs[name], then calls client.close() — lines 625-630
connect_disconnect.finalizer: Effect.addFinalizer that kills child processes (via SIGTERM), closes all clients, and clears pendingOAuthTransports — lines 598-618

builtin_tools.pattern: Tool.init(toolInstance) → stored in flat object → spread into builtin array

plugin_tools.pattern: for each plugin: plugin.checkCapability('tool.register') → fromPlugin(id, def) → custom.push()

user_file_tools.pattern: Glob.scanSync('{tool,tools}/*.{js,ts}') → dynamic import → fromPlugin(id, def) → custom.push()

mcp_tools.pattern: listTools(client) → convertMcpTool() → s.defs[name] = listed → mcp.tools() returns Record<string, ai.Tool>

no_runtime_register.note: ToolRegistry interface has NO addTool/register method. All built-in and plugin tool registration happens once at layer construction time. There is no runtime tool addition/removal API exposed from ToolRegistry.
no_runtime_register.mcp_exception: MCP.Service has add/connect/disconnect for runtime MCP server management, but these don't interact with ToolRegistry.

tool_registry.no_per_tool_unregister: No unregister, removeTool, or deregister method exists.
tool_registry.scoped_disposal: Tool state lives in InstanceState (ScopedCache keyed by directory). When the Effect Scope closes or InstanceStore.dispose(ctx.directory) is called, the cache is invalidated and tools are garbage collected.
tool_registry.disposer_registration: registerDisposer(directory => ScopedCache.invalidate(cache, directory)) — line 49-51 of instance-state.ts

mcp.disconnect: closeClient(s, name) — deletes s.defs[name], calls client.close() — line 625-630
mcp.disconnect_service_method: disconnect(name) — acquire lock, require config, closeClient, delete s.clients/status — line 712-723
mcp.finalizer_cleanup: Effect.addFinalizer kills child processes (SIGTERM), closes ALL clients, clears pendingOAuthTransports — line 598-618
mcp.no_individual_tool_removal: MCP tools can only be removed by disconnecting the entire server, not individually.

instance_store_dispose.note: InstanceStore.dispose() calls InstanceRegistry.disposeInstance() which runs all registered disposers, including the ScopedCache invalidations for both ToolRegistry and MCP state.

tool_registry_as_effect_service.default_layer: exported as defaultLayer with ~20 Layer.provide dependencies

session_tools_not_a_service.yes: SessionTools is a namespace, NOT an Effect Service. It depends on ToolRegistry and MCP being in the Effect context.
session_tools_not_a_service.context_requirements: ToolRegistry.Service, MCP.Service, Plugin.Service, Permission.Service, Truncate.Service, EffectBridge, Option<PhaseGate.Service>, Option<ToolCache.Service>

effect_service_wrapping.no_dual_registration: Tools are NOT registered in both ToolRegistry and MCP. Built-in/plugin tools → ToolRegistry. External MCP tools → MCP.Service. They converge only in SessionTools.resolve() which iterates both and produces a unified Record<string, ai.Tool>.

consumers_of_tool_registry.role: AppLayer composition — ToolRegistry.defaultLayer provided at top level
SessionPrompt.defaultLayer — replumbed at line 118, overridden via Effect.provideService at lines 1387-1400
SessionTools.resolve() — main consumer, reads all tools for LLM calls

from_plugin_converter.description: Converts ToolDefinition (from plugins or user files) into Tool.Def. Handles Zod→JSONSchema conversion, legacy shape compatibility, and wraps execution with error handling + span annotation.
key_details: Legacy plain-object args → legacyJsonSchema for backward compat (line 342)
Wraps execute in Effect.gen with: plugin trigger('tool.execute.before'), promiseBridge, error→TypedResult.failure catch, plugin trigger('tool.execute.after'), Effect.withSpan

smoking_guns.finding: ToolRegistry has no runtime add/remove API
smoking_guns.detail: The Interface (line 172-180) has no register, unregister, addTool, or removeTool method. All tool registration is done at layer initialization time via InstanceState.make. This means tools cannot be dynamically added after the layer is built.

MCP tools are stored in MCP's own InstanceState (defs: Record<string, MCPToolDef[]>) and surfaced via mcp.tools(). SessionTools.resolve() has two separate loops — one for registry.tools() (built-in) and one for mcp.tools() (MCP). There is no unified tool registry, no shared tool ID namespace, and no cross-cutting visibility between the two domains.

smoking_guns.finding: No tool deduplication across built-in and MCP
smoking_guns.detail: Built-in tools use snake_case IDs (e.g. 'read_source', 'smart_bash'), MCP tools use sanitize(clientName)_sanitize(toolName). If an MCP server exposes a tool with a sanitized name that collides with a builtin tool ID, the MCP tool will silently overwrite the builtin in the SessionTools.resolve() output (because it uses tools[key] = item assignment).

smoking_guns.finding: Tools are scoped to instance directory, not session
smoking_guns.detail: ToolRegistry state is stored in InstanceState (keyed by directory via ScopedCache). Multiple sessions within the same directory share the same ToolRegistry state. Disposal is instance-scoped, not session-scoped.

smoking_guns.finding: User-file tools are loaded via dynamic import at layer init
smoking_guns.detail: Lines 398-417 use pathToFileURL + import() to load user tool files. These files are not watched for changes — a restart/reload is required to pick up new or modified tool files.

provided_at: consumed via yield* TypedResult.wrapExecute() in SessionTools.resolve

mcp_registration_chain.detail: MCP.layer reads config.mcp, iterates entries, calls create(name, mcpConfig) for each
connectLocal() spawns StdioClientTransport child process; connectRemote() uses StreamableHTTPClientTransport/SSEClientTransport
defs(name, client, timeout) calls client.listTools() via MCP SDK, caches raw MCPToolDef[] in State.defs[name]
convertMcpTool(mcpTool, client, timeout) wraps in ai dynamicTool with JSONSchema7 input schema and client.callTool() execute
storeClient(s,name,client,listed,timeout) stores client+defs+status, starts watch() for ToolListChangedNotification → auto-reloads defs
mcp.tools() iterates connected clients, reads s.defs[name], converts each via convertMcpTool(). Key = sanitize(clientName)+_+sanitize(toolName)
SessionTools.resolve() iterates mcp.tools() (line 159 of session/tools.ts) and merge-enters them into the same Record<string,ai.Tool> as builtins
